tokens: And give us a mark when you're going.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: We've been going about a minute.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And we don't have battery temperatures there, if you could quickly give them to us.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Okay, I never did give you that.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Never did get that for you.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: But I tell you, the inner wall of Camelot to the east is certainly blocky.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, there were a few blocks where you guys were, too.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Yeah, well, I don't know, you could see the other wall, too.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, and, 17, what we're looking for is deploying charge number eight at (bearing) zero eight two and zero decimal four on the range.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: We're just about abeam the eastern rim of Camelot.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You can even see the ALSEP.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I think I'll go this way.
(Jack laughs as Gene avoids a crater; Pause.)
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Gee, it seems like a short day.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, I'll tell you, the time went fast.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (To Bob) Okay, you've heard about this country (on the outbound trip to Station 2).
(Pause) Hey, looking over there, though, there's no...We're about, oh, 50 meters from boulders in Camelot.
And their appearance from this distance is the same as what we sampled at (Station) 5.
I think we've pretty well identified the subfloor, Bob.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, sounds like we have and sounds like from the very deepest - even from the bottom of Camelot - it looks like it's about the same.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Brief static) I can't say I understand it.
But that's the way it appears right now.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I can't say that I understand.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Whatever filled this valley, it certainly was different than the Massifs.
At least everything I see indicates that it was an igneous extrusion of some kind.

Either that, or the whole valley's been tilted and we're looking at some strange cross section - planar, more or less, relative to the other mountains - of a crystalline body that was formed at depth.
But I don't think that's likely.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Is that what we planned to cover?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: What did we plan to cover, Bob?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: But we've been everywhere we've planned to go, so, we must have gone just about as far as we've planned to go.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Yeah, but it's a straight line.
I don't know what the wander factor was, but I'm sure it was pretty high.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: So we went a little bit out of our way.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: 082, I think, but we want oh-point-four.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: But you want to bear north, don't you, a little?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Yeah, I want...What'd you say, 082, didn't you, Bob?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: It's probably be right on your track, there.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Well, I don't make a habit of following myself.
I like to cover new ground.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Watch out for that new ground there.
Looks like in the...Look at the Italian flag!
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I saw the box before I saw the flag.
(Pause) No, I didn't, I saw the flag first, I've got to admit it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I got to admit it, I saw the flag first.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: The boss said you saw the flag first.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'll just head right in towards the LM.
Man, I want to stay away from ALSEP, I see the big boulder (Geophone Rock), so I'll...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, you'll have to swing (to the) right after we deploy, probably.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Did we ever get any glass out of the bottom of those craters?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: No, we haven't, we've got to try to do that before we leave.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Yeah, sometime you'll have time to do that, I guess, guys.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Okay, let's put it in that little depression there.
See right ahead of us to the right?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Now just swing into that depression and I'll put it there.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: You didn't get a (locator) picture to the LM then, did you?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, we don't have to take any more, do we?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I guess as long as it didn't go off, it's safe.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (They both laugh) I don't care what they say, that's a charge.
(Pause) You're having great luck with those.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, the LM was in the approach shot, I believe.
Let me...Go ahead and turn around.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Yeah, I got to go around anyway.
(Pause) This way I can get a running shot of (static).
(Garbled) right in the middle of it.
Let me get them both in it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, I ran out of film, too.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: When you come around, take a picture of the LM on your camera.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'll take it right out the front looking right at the thing.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Yeah, and give them a frame count.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Pause) Bob, I've got the "locator" of the charge and the LM all in the same order here, and I'm one more than what I just gave you.
I can't look at it now.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, one more than what you gave me at Station 5 (which was frame 50).
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: You want me to go to the gravimeter now?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And if you guys will start out, we'll drive you by the ALSEP, Jack, and if you'll get out at the ALSEP, we'll have you take a look at the surface gravimeter and Gene can press on home to the LM.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Jack, I'm going to drive you in this way, and then I'll drive all the way back around that one geophone.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Garbled) to the north, you could drive in toward the heat flow, towards that big rock, if you can see that.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Yeah, well, okay; that's as good as anything.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Bob, you want me to get some ALSEP pictures?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Pause) Okay, and Jack, you can stand by...
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Okay, (PLSS) Feedwater, go to Aux(illiary), please.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Man, look at that mess of cable.
I hope that thing (the ALSEP)'s working, Bob.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Every...Well, a lot of it's working.
We've just got to try and see if we can't level this thing (the LSG) tonight.
When you get off there, Jack, I'll talk to you a bit about procedures for that.
And in the meanwhile, it's my understanding...
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: ...that the second UHT is not in the immediate vicinity, where it's accessible, is that right?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: No, it is, I can get it.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, and watch my heat flow (cables) over there, don't trip over them (as happened on Apollo 16).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, I'm going to take a pair of tongs.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Do you have any film at all?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, Jack, you won't need (a camera)...We aren't planning on taking the ALSEP photos right now.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: And, Jack, we're not sure you went to Aux on your water yet.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Responding to Bob) I thought I did.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, it's coming up, now, we see it coming, Jack, don't worry.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Gene, can you...(Responding to Bob) Okay.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Let me take a set of tongs in case I drop something.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (To Bob) Okay, Jack's got my camera and tongs, and I'm on my way.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, Bob, I got a tone again.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Probably just your water (garbled) been building up...
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Well, we better take a look.
Okay, I'm coming over there (to the Rover).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I think I just got my water tone, Bob.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, not unless...Let me see, let me brush your...Bend over.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You don't have a brush to brush off (because it is under Gene's seat).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You look good to us, Jack.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Jack, you look good to us.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Okay, and, Gene, I think you need to go to Aux...
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Yeah, I just did, Bob, and my water flag cleared.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Yeah, it's probably still coming up.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Okay, Jack, just be careful of the cables.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, you want me to get a UHT, huh?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: It's right over there by the...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Yeah, I know where it is...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: If it's quite close, but if it's not, don't bother, I think we can probably try this by hand.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: No, it's there, Bob; he'll get it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (To Bob) You don't want me to kick the LEAM yet, huh?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, Bob, I've got a UHT.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, when you go back to the LSG, Jack, we first of all would like a reading on where the bubble is in the circle, (that is) whether it's in the center.
And what we're going to have to have, unfortunately, is to have the bubble centered in the inner circle (pause) because this is, apparently, a requirement, even though we didn't train to it.
Apparently it's something that's come up, and it's going to have to be within the inner circle of the bubble.
So, the first thing we need to do is, when you go up there, is to see whether the bubble is in the center of the circle.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Okay, the next thing we want you to do is put the UHT in the socket there, and move the...
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I did not have to touch it, it is centered.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, but now they're worried that it's stuck like your gimbal thing was last night (on the Central Station antenna).
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: And, also, the thing is that maybe something's hung up inside and, by moving it, we can jostle it free.
They do not want it picked up, but they'd like to have UHT put in there and sort of have the instrument rocked from side to side to again see the bubble move.
And once that is done, to then press it down into the ground and again re-align it and put the bubble within the inner circle.
They would also like to see if the gimbal is free.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: How much do you want me to rock it?
How far should I let the bubble travel?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Basically, we just want to see it move.
(If) you go out to the outer circle, that's plenty.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Oh, I mean the outer rim of it.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: It's still centered and gimbal is swinging.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, we copy the gimbal is swinging and we copy you moved the bubble out to the edge of the bubble level and come back into the inner circle.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Puffing rather heavily as he leans forward) Okay, Bob, I'm reading 089, 20.1, 002, 92, 88.
Forward left is off-scale low and right is 210 degrees.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: What's the first battery temperature there, Gene?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Unenthusiastically passing up the request) Okay, Jack.
They apparently don't believe you when you said you aligned it last night and they're concerned the shade is not on 20 degrees.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Copy 114 on that battery temperature, Gene.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Okay; in that case, I guess you're free to come home.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Well, what is basically the problem with it?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: They haven't been able to level it for some reason, and they were afraid that the thing wasn't level.
They were hoping also maybe by moving it that you might jostle it a bit and it would come to level, but I think we'll just have to think about it some more.
Their first presumption - the easiest solution - was to have it unleveled, which case we could fix it this way.
But I guess that's not the case.
We'll have to see what happens overnight.
Give us a chance to follow the tradition of coming back to the ALSEP tomorrow.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Well, do you want me to change it's level a little bit...put it off level a little and see if you can work it.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: That doesn't sound very good to me.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Well, it might be some air (or error?) in the level bubble or something.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Why don't you just leave it there if it's centered with the level bubble within the inner circle there.
That's the requirements as far as we can tell, and we'll just have to leave it overnight again.
And why don't you come on back to the LM.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Okay, Gene, are you at the Rover?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: You gave me your readings and when you get done, let me know, because the first thing we want to do is work on the SEP (receiver) a little bit.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: What do you want to do to the SEP?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: When you go back to the SEP now - and let's do this first and get it out of the way - you can probably do this anyway while Jack's coming home.

When you open the blankets, remember that at the back of the SEP there was a piece of Velcro on the case and a piece of Velcro just inside the rear hinge on the covers, and this is what you sort of peel back when you go to remove the back end there so you can get the DSEA out?
You remember that piece of tape there?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: This is on the side away from you as you stand facing it.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: That's the side...Go ahead, Gene, I'm sorry.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Gene, if you stand facing it, in the back - away from you - there's a strip of Velcro on the case and there's another strip of Velcro on the blanket itself and this is the Velcro you have to tear off or to unhook, as it were, when you come to tear the blankets down to get the DSEA off.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Yeah, but that's on the side away from me.
I'm on the LMP's side and that's on the other side.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: That's on the other side of the top.
And what we're interested in, number 1, is that piece of Velcro still mated or when you open the blankets to cool it, does that Velcro come open?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Well, that's the Velcro that's so full of dust it comes open, Bob.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Trying to stay...It's hooked; it'll stay.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I don't know if you can see that but it's...Well, you can't (because of the) back seat...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Bob, I swung the covers and they will stay open about 150 degrees.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Trying to figure out what Gene's saying) I...Stand by a minute.
What you're saying is that the cover actually stands up a little bit in space.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Now, if you want the cover open, I can open it and normally it should swing 180 degrees, (that is) parallel with the top of the SEP.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, but does that Velcro in back stay mated?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Well, the Velcro holds (and), the way that we've been opening it, it holds.
(Hearing Bob) Yes, it's being mated and probably it holds the covers open 150 degrees!
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: What the concern originally was, was that when this...
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: ...happened, the sunlight was getting down in the back there and warming up the back of the SEP.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: The Velcro that came off is the Velcro that keeps the covers closed.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We were concerned about both pieces of Velcro since the one had come off.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: The other one is still on and it still holds and the back part of the SEP - where the DSEA is - is in the shade.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: While we're talking about this - and I'll get back with you - turn the DSEA and the receiver both to Off, please.
(Pause) And read me a temperature, please.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: And I mark them both off, right?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Pause) Bob, I just dusted it as clean as it'll get.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I just sampled the glass in the bottom of a crater.
I documented it by shooting the LM across the crater at infinity and then shooting the crater with stereo at 11 feet and then a cross-Sun pair at 7; and then I sampled it.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Then I took a cross-Sun (stereo)pair at 7 (foot focus) "after".
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Punning) I guess now "gnomon is a LM".
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: It's very fragile, and I double bagged it.
I don't know whether we can keep it or not.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: You may think about how to preserve it.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: While you're thinking, I'll put it on my (Rover) floor pan, I guess.
What do I have to do here?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Get this bag (SCB) off me to start.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Get that bag off you to start with.
(Pause) Be careful of that sample there.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Those are the cleanest battery covers in existence on a Rover right now, I tell you.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Noticing that Fendell is tilting the TV down to look) Oh, you don't believe me.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Noticing that the TV is still pointed down) Now you're stuck down there, aren't you?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Seems like you (would think) I may be going nuts talking to that moving machine (the TV) over there.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Boy, we don't need any hooks; I'll tell you.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Can you stoop just a little bit?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Yeah, all that stuff is getting...There (garbled).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Garbled) get your hook (perhaps the Velcro strap that secures the SCB at the bottom)'s back.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Yeah, hook my harness back up so we don't forget that.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You know, (as) you look at those little sparklies in the soil we're walking on and they change colors on you.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'll come over, and I'll unload your stuff.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Now, we got more samples than we've got sense, I think.
Let's see here (consulting checklist), you're taking care of the SEP.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Hey, Bob; the covers are open on the SEP.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Oh, why did I do that?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Hey, Bob, you think that glass sample would be better off in the SRC?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Your core cap assembly is empty.
(Probably throwing it away) Up and away it goes!
Now, Bob, you've got to tell us which one of these (SCBs) you want in the SCB (means SRC) and which one you want just taken in because we got our numbers all confused.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: What we would like to do here...
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Let me read this to you first of all.
In the SRC, we'd like the following stuff along with...Let's see...Stand by.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: We're going to follow an Apollo 16 mode and put stuff in (the SRC) loose, because they'd like to segregate stuff in the following way.
We'd like to put the long can and four core tubes in the SRC.
I guess it's going to take a while just carrying stuff back and forth (between the Rover and the MESA).
But they'd probably like to get this in because of the volatile stuff.
They'd like to get the long can and three core tubes in the SRC; (that's priority) number 1.
And then we'd like to get all the SCB-4 samples in the same SRC.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: ...three plus the long can; that's four cores all together.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You want to get the core tubes in first, though.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I want to put these in.
If you'd give me the...Yeah, I only got two hands.
I'll come back by the time you dig them out.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, and then, 17, do you guys remember where the trench samples - the three trench soil samples - which bag (SCB) those were put in?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I'm the only one who had (sample) bags, so I bagged them and put them in whatever bag Jack had (that is, SCB-4).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: So we'd like those in SCB-4...those are the ones that will go in the rock box and that's in agreement with what we want to do.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Okay, give me those other two cores, if you've got them, Jack.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Yeah, and we need one more core.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Three core tubes and a long can?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And then all the samples in SCB-4.
We won't bother to try and sort them out, and then beyond that we'll fill them up with samples from SCB-5.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Well, it's on the gate right there, just hanging.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Going to the gate) Four is the one I had on there at Shorty?
Or you had on at Shorty?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I don't know, but they should have that logged.
I don't remember who had it on.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And I put them in you.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Did I have 4 on at Shorty?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: That's why we want SCB-4 dumped into the thing (the SRC).
But it's a dirty bag, so we just want to dump the samples in.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Okay; and you want 5 to fill it up.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And, Jack, it probably would protect the glass a bit better if you put it in the SRC gently with the other rocks there.
Particularly if you don't fill the SRC too full.
But, again, we'll be putting SCB (5) samples in there to more or less flesh it out if there's not too many SCB-4 samples.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Leave a space for a sample, I guess, Gene.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, you'd better give it to me.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You're really...Well, can you leave one...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: No, I'll bring it to you.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: It's in the right-hand back corner of the SRC.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: You got some (samples) left in there (SCB-5)?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'll get some small ones and some big ones, too.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pause) Hey, we got some big rock samples.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You should also have SCB-8 under your seat with samples in it.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: This is what I sampled at...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Six has the samples from...(Hearing Bob) Yes.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We have more samples today than Carter...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And let's take up SCB-6; and why don't you dump out the Rover samples into SCB-6?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Well, one reason not to take 6 is I don't know if I can get it off (the Rover)!
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And let's save SCB-4 because I think you may need that tomorrow.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Four is on the rack, empty.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Is that only partially emptied, or is it totally emptied?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: No, it's about half full, Bob.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We'll take that up with us.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Bob, I've already...Let me tell you what I've done.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: It's got Rover samples in it.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: But I can't get them all.
They won't all be in there.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Bob, the seal (on the box) was clean.
It was clear, and I got your four cores...(correcting himself) three cores, plus a long can.
I got SCB-4 and a couple of samples out of SCB-5.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You got me all out of whack, here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: "Core cap dispenser." Okay, you're "clean" (and the) cosmic ray is done.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Now, Jack, we've got SCB-5 that's half full.
What have you got over there?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Bring it over here, and I'll put it into 6.
Six is a little more than half full.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, this is a little less than half full.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: That ought to make one full bag.
And these are big rocks so they'll come out easy.
Where's that big, big rock we got?
That's in one of those bags, too.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Here let me see if I can't dump it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: How's that for a lunar dump?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Going to the gate) I want to see if I can't dust (the bag latches on the gate)...Did you lock this one over here?...No.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: No, I'm going to see if I can dust them and make them work easier.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Boy, I'll tell you...I really had to pull.
I pulled harder than I like to in a pressure suit.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, and I understand 5 will be...
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Stowing the empty SCB) And 7 under the LMP's seat.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: 4 and 5 will be on the gate.
You know, here's a problem for you tonight.
You got any way of freeing up these gate hinges that lock the bags on?
I'm dusting them, but they're not going to lock, any of them.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: If you do get them locked, you may never get them off.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We'll give them something to work on overnight.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Okay, I'm dusting them right now.
But I still can't free them up.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Jack, have you got the top of the bag closed?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Returning to the Rover) Yeah, what do you need?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: John (Young) thought maybe they were still open.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I'll check them again before I go up.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, and we got no FSRs (football-sized rocks) underneath the seat.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I just checked and they're all in the bags.
And the Rover sample bag is empty.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Bob, neither one of these bag latchers are going to latch on the back.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Yeah, we can probably use the seat.
We've got a little more room than we had.
Let me get something else done.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Taking a packet of sample bags from under his seat) I wonder if I ought to take a sample bag holder up there to see if I can fix that.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: He mumbled something very humbly about a thousand guys.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Well, tell him that's going to be my "bring home" present to him: a picture of his fender.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Unused gear - which you have an inventory on (by a process of elimination) - is under the LMP's seat.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Taking the gravimeter to the ladder) I'm bringing the
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Man, I got the sorest hands in the world, right now.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Carrying the ETB to the Rover) Bum, bum, bum, baa.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Nothing's in the big bag, is it?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Responding to Bob) I just can't compete with you astronomers.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Nothing's in the bag, is it?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Unless there's one rock in there that disappeared yesterday.
I don't know what happened to it.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Hey, we forgot the polarizing filter work!
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Nah, I saw it on the checklist, and I mentioned it to Bob, and he didn't come back with anything.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Put those (unused sample bags) under that seat, please.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (At Schmitt's seat) Yeah, there's only one left.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Who knows, we may need them at the rate we're going.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And, Jack, while you're unloading there,...
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: ...on the 500 millimeter you might squeeze off a few shots of the North and South Massif there, if there's any lineations visible.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Why, are we ahead of time?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: No, we're working right on time.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Why don't you give it to me while you're packing the ETB, Jack; I'll do it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Am I behind you, now (in the checklist)?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Yeah, I'm going to start inventorying the Rover and pulling the breakers so...(Pause)
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Man, we are so far off nominal on what bags (SCBs we've used)...
The checklist is going to have to be updated, I guess.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Oh, I should call (and tell Houston what I'm putting in the ETB).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And tell Gene that we can confirm that his lens cover's off.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (To Gene) They confirm that your lens cover's off.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Facing east) I hope this is the right setting.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Laughs) Hey, try f/5.6 directly down-Sun...(correcting himself) or, up-Sun, at (the) Sculptured Hills there in the distance.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (To himself) What else do I need here?
(To Gene) (I) need mag R as soon as you're through.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I think we've got enough of those now, Gene.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Some of these (photos) won't overlap, Bob, because I'm hurrying.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: They're not smeared, but I just didn't overlap some of them.
(Jack laughs) I'll get those others, Jack, tomorrow.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You got the maps in there, too, Jack?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Do you have the maps there, Jack?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I guess I'll go in and pull some Rover breakers.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Leaning inboard over the Rover) Oh, boy.
Pulling breakers is not going to be much fun.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You want me to...You want the scissors or something (to use in pulling the breakers)?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Laughing) You ask me that one more time Parker, and I'm going to get mad at ya.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: What have you got over there?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I got three of four breakers.
Let me try this other one one more time.
I got it - with the old fingers.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: All four of them out, Gene.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Alpha, Bravo, Charlie, Delta are Open.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And, Gene, when you leave the (TV) camera, a reminder to face it away from the Sun and tilt it down.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: That's not quite away from the Sun.
Really it ought to be, you know, down-Sun.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: See, I just turned LCRU power off; they got to go through the LM.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Otherwise, I'll turn this power back on.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Read you loud and clear, Gene.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Well, let me turn it on and talk to them.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We read you loud and clear, Gene.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Well, I just turned the LCRU back on.
Are you reading us through the LM, now?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Yeah, we came through the LM that time.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I'm turning it off and the camera is pointed down and it's pointed effectively to the west.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Uh-oh, I got to work on one battery.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, they're supposed to be going through the LM.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pause) Maybe we got the switches wrong, or something, up there (in the LM cabin.) Don't think so, though.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Let me give them a call.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Bob, you want to try again.
(Hearing Bob) Yeah, we read you, but I'm on the LCRU again.
We're not reading you through the LM!
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Brief pause) Press on with the rest of the close-out.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Well, I'll leave you on the LCRU, here.
(Responding to Bob) Yeah, we're pressing on.
For the first time, I've got to dust the center battery cover.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, yeah, but I'm on the LCRU.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: No, now they say we're going back to the LM again.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I'm going to go turn the LCRU power Off.
And for the first time, I've got to dust the center radiator on the batteries.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Up until this time, they've all been real clean.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And in the interim I'm going to turn the LCRU Power, Off.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We've got about two-zero (20) minutes before we have to be inside the LM there, fellows.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Joking) Oh, I think we'll just sort of take it easy, Bob.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I've got the (PLSS LiOH) canisters; pins are green.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: The LM (lithium hydroxide) canister's in the pocket (on the EVA pallet).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: No, I'm leaving here right now.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And, Gene, as you go by, how about giving us the SEP temperature readings.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Garbled) I got to take these bags up.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: About one hundred and...About 108 to (1)10 degrees.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We'll leave it there, as is, overnight.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: And I'll give her one good little smack with the brush.
And it's as clean as it'll ever come.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Jack, you might just as well go cold water.
There's no more use for it now, if you're warm.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: No, I'm not warm I'm just...
Want to hand me that other SCB there?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: The cover's open on this one.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You wonder why it's hard to get up the ladder.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Don't hold it by the cover.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (To himself) What do I want these tongs on for?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Don't want them (up to the cabin).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Don't take it by the cover.
The (SCB) cover's going to come open.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pause) Probably got tongs on for the same reason I've got them on.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And, 17, we're ready for a grav measurement.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Getting our tongs out of the way (and back on the gate).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And for your information, at this heading (of 017), the westernmost battery cover, like I talked about yesterday, is just starting to cover the radiators.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay; cover that...(Correcting himself) Copy that.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: You know, I think it's another good day's work.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I just knocked as hard as I could, on my feet, several times.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: What you're going to say is you want me to brush you, huh?
Let me keep this (gravimeter) out of the way so it doesn't get dusty.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Wonder if our bouncing around that gravimeter is going to disturb it like this.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You got to do me first.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You're not nearly as dusty as you were yesterday; you're just dirty, that's all.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I think I can get everything off my shoes by banging, if you'll just get my arms.
I didn't really fall in much today, except maybe my left arm.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I tell you, (if) we saw some of the things I think we saw today, we both fell in.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: That's the way mine (his arm) feels.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pause) I'm glad they can't see this.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Oh, I stand out here and I look at that flag, and I look at the Rover, and I look at those massifs.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: What did we deserve to do...(correcting himself) do to deserve being out here, huh?
Let me get some (of the dust on) your PLSS, here.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Hey, that fender is really a classic.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Punning) One might say it's a "young" fender, (because we) just put it on.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Do we need...Do we really need those clamps?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I can't think of anything we need them for.
That light...but you can tie that light somewhere...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I might bring one back (in the LM, just in case).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We ought to leave one in tribute.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Oh, that orange soil was something.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And the way it went radially down that crater.
(Pause) Let me turn, and then take another look, and then I'll...Then you get up there.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You got quite a bit (of dust) around your hoses here.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, and I don't know what I can do about it, Geno.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Oh, just give it a swat (garbled) front, too.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, I got it the best I could.
Let me get the top of your LCRU there.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Let me get to your front.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: No, I didn't get your other arm here.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I don't know that I can.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I don't have anything on it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: When you fall out, you fall out on your other arm.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: There is some on that leg there.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Don't know what I'm going to do about it.
(Pause) I think we're just going to have to make do.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Let me see what I can do with you.
(Pause) What is this rock, right here, by the pad!
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Did I just turn your comm?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I've just been intending to mention that (rock) several times.
Anybody that lands on a rock ought to have their head examined.
Put their ladder (strut right on a rock)...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Boy, where have you been again today?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Oh, I played some games there around Station 3.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Lot of your turns threw dust...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: If I was strong enough, I'd move it.
That's one we ought to bring home!
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, if we can't fill up the LM with anything else.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: That's about the size of the SRC.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You know, by rights, that (meaning dropping the dustbrush) should happen more.
(Pause) I want to get around back and then I want you to get up on the ladder some (so I can dust your legs.) Oh, man, let me get the back of your PLSS.
I guess that's from the Rover seat.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: No, I mean you got a couple abrasions right on through the...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I wasn't going to mention them (and risk alarming anybody in Houston).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, these are only a pin-hole thick, but...(Pause)
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: All I can say is it's better than walking.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Oh, man, I'll tell you, we covered over 20 kilometers today, babe.
How'd you like to walk up and down those hills and ditches at 20 kilometers?
I want to get the front of your legs one time.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Man, you've had your day of dusting, haven't you?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'm going to send the LMP in in about a minute.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We're getting to a point where we need...
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Oh, I tell you, I've spent more hours on the
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: ...where we need to be inside in less than 10 minutes, with the thing (the hatch) closed up.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (To Jack) That takes care of it.
Knock (the dust off of) your feet off, Jack.
Knock your feet off on the ladder.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And don't forget the antennas, guys.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Responding to Bob) Oh, Bob, you're a beauty.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: How did you think of that?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (To Jack) You'd (have) broke yours off sure, because I wouldn't have seen it as you went up (pause) unless I took inventory with my book, which I'll do here in a minute.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: If I can just get up here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I forgot a snap up there (on the antenna tie-down).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Now, let's make sure we got all of those.
I don't want to get hung up on anything.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I mean I forgot to put the antenna under the snap...under the other thing.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Wait a minute, get the snap.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Go on up, Jack, and I'm going to read the gravimeter.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I need that pallet before I go.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, and, Gene, we don't think you've punched the Grav reading yet.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I'm going to read it for you in a minute.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Open the cover and dust the (
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: It's dusted already; I took care of that.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: The big bag is not required.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pause) And we're ready to call you all in as you go through the hatch.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Jack's going in with the...(Hearing Bob) Okay, Jack's halfway through it now, and I'm going on up the ladder.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: That's a nice one-handed first step.
I'm going to keep reading that.
(Calling to Bob) How's Captain America (meaning Ron Evans), speaking of Apollo 17?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Just about to come around to AOS (Acquisition of Signal when the Command Module emerges from behind the Moon on the current orbit).
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Hey, how does that always happen?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: He got up before noon this morning, too.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Pause) Just take it easy, Jack, it'll...Here you go...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Lot of things don't work when your hands get tired; that's the problem.
How long we been out, Bob.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pause) Well, we're getting anxious to get you in and get the hatch closed.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Garbled under Bob) Well, we understand that.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Jack's unloading the pallet and as soon as it comes out, I'll shove this stuff (the SRC and the two SCBs) in, and we'll be gone.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Just takes a certain amount of time.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Are the three SCBs inside the hatch already?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Bob, I don't think any of them are inside.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: ...I've got 8 here and 6 here and we emptied the contents of 4 into the...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: ...SRC, and we emptied the contents of 5 into one of these other two bags.
So we've only got two of them here, plus the SRC.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And we've got two of them hanging on the tail of the Rover.
And I don't know what it is under Jack's seat right now.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: One more (PLSS) battery (to get off the pallet), Geno, and it's yours.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: You always used to stand and watch me do this, anyway.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Yeah, but we had some long EVAs at the Cape but...(Pause)
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Just be careful of the...Let me get up there a little farther.
Getting to look like a regular garbage dump down there.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Now, this one (an SCB)'s going to come open if we're not careful.
Let me see if I've got one more step to go up.
No I think that's the last one.
I'll just hold it here until you get it.
(Pause) I could shove that in if I push it with the SRC.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Can you push on that (box) a little bit?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, Bob, you've got the two SCBs - (To Jack) I'll push it in - (the) SRC, and there goes the ETB.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: There's only one thing left...(Hearing Bob) That's right, that's the only thing left out here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: No, I'm not even on my checklist, but I guess...Yeah, I am; it says "Ingress".
Let me knock some more dust off.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Let me get behind the door.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, I'm going to take what dust I got in with me.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pause) Keep your buttons close (to the floor).
Hanging up a little on the
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: That's my arm I'm getting in the way, there.
Let me get out of the way.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Let me just check that (hatch) seal (for dust) before we close that.
Can you get your arm up?
There's no big rocks in it (the hatch seal), that I can see.
Lots of dust on the floor.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, the hatch is partially closed...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I think it (the checklist) says to lock it, doesn't it?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, we're supposed to close our water first.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Here, why don't I get yours and you get mine.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And if you'll turn, I'll get yours.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You'll have to put your right arm high; pick it up high.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: No, I can't reach you, Jack, unless you turn...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Don't move yet; don't turn around.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Make) sure I got the right ones.
Far right, far left, secure oxygen.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Now, stay there and I'll lock the hatch.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I've got to get into position to do the other good things (that is, close circuit breakers on the panel on the right side of the cabin and reset
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: No, let me get down there.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Let me turn back (and face inboard); get out of your way.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And remember, I think it's the overhead valve that you have (garbled) Open.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Yeah, you'll have to move over, Jack, so I can reach it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And you ought to verify both, I guess.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (To Jack) You're going to have to wait, then.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: No, you got to swing left...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'll have to turn around, then.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, for some reason I can't put my PLSS toward you.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Just face front, if you can, and move as far forward as you can.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, if I get my...I'm going to have to go all the way around, I guess.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Look, I've got to get the PLSS against the circuit breaker (panel)...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I've got you just where I want you.
Now stay right there, and shift your weight as far to the right (probably forward) as you can.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And at (panel) 16, Cabin Repress breaker, Closed.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Master Alarm and Cabin Warning Lights.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I've still got 15 percent oxygen (left in the PLSS).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And your PLSS O2 (oxygen), Off, at cabin (pressure) greater (than) 2.5 (psi).
And you're probably getting (a) soft suit.
transcript.speaker: Cernan
